label: Word formation
description: Making words from words: the alterati (diminutives, augmentatives, pejoratives), the intensifying prefixes, and the fakes.

label: Alterati
description: Suffixes that resize and colour a noun without changing what it is.

label: Diminutives: -ino, -etto, -ello, -uccio
description: Small and often affectionate: tavolino, momentino, sorellina (affection, not necessarily age). Suffix choice is partly lexicalised; produce the common ones, recognise the rest.

attributes.canonical_example: Un attimino! Sento la mancanza della mia sorellina.
attributes.common_miss: Free-generating on any noun (not every noun takes every suffix).

label: Augmentative -one, pejorative -accio
description: Big (librone, portone) and bad (tempaccio, parolaccia). Watch the gender shift: feminine bases often go masculine under -one (la porta, il portone; la campana, il campanello).

attributes.canonical_example: Che tempaccio! Hanno chiuso il portone.
attributes.common_miss: la portona (missed gender shift); -accio read as size.

label: Falsi alterati
description: Words that only LOOK altered: cavalletto is an easel not a small horse; mattino is not a small matto; focaccia is not a bad foca. Classification is the skill: real alterato or independent word?

label: Stra-, arci-, super-, ultra-
description: Intensifying prefixes on adjectives: strapieno, arcistufo, superveloce. They form absolute superlatives without -issimo (cite the comparison topic for that frame).

attributes.common_miss: stra- on verbs/nouns where it does not go; missing that arcistufo IS a superlative.

label: Neo-, ex-
description: neo- = newly (neolaureato, neonato, neologismo); ex = former (il mio ex marito, written separate or hyphenated). Recognition first, production second.

attributes.common_miss: neo- read as a meaning change rather than newness.

label: Relational adjectives (stub)
description: Noun-derived adjectives (-ale, -are, -istico, -oso, -ivo: nazione > nazionale, sole > solare) and the irregular Latinate pairs (fegato > epatico). Stubbed: authored later, possibly with the adjective topic.

label: -one gender shift (la porta, il portone)
description: Feminine bases that turn masculine under the accrescitivo -one: la porta > il portone, la palla > il pallone, la stanza > lo stanzone. The diagnostic is the article/gender, distinct from producing the -one suffix itself.
